narratorStyle: Be direct and rough around the edges. These are dark times in a galaxy mired in despair, but even so there are bright spots and moments of levity and campy humor to be found, and more often, moments of longing for a better life in a better world. Use mostly plain, common words. One good adjective beats three. Short sentences are the default. Fragments are fine. A long sentence earns its length with momentum, not stacked clauses. One vivid detail beats three generic ones. The specific detail is always better than the label. Get in, make it land, get out. Build scenes from people, actions, and things that happen. Don't summarize states. The prose should feel like a story told aloud. No word exists to impress. Every word exists to communicate.
